steps.title: Factor out the common factor in the numerator
steps.content:
In the numerator $8x(x-7) - 3(x-7)$, both terms contain $(x-7)$ as a factor. Factor it out:

$$8x(x-7) - 3(x-7) = (x-7)(8x-3)$$

The denominator factors as:

$$2x - 14 = 2(x-7)$$

So the original expression becomes:

$$\frac{(x-7)(8x-3)}{2(x-7)}$$

steps.title: Cancel the common factor and simplify
steps.content:
Since $x > 7$, we know $x - 7 \neq 0$, so the $(x-7)$ factor cancels:

$$\frac{(x-7)(8x-3)}{2(x-7)} = \frac{8x-3}{2}$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
